rule.desc: Enable `strict: true` in `tsconfig.json` for all projects.
rule.rationale: Strict mode enables `strictNullChecks`, `noImplicitAny`, `strictFunctionTypes`, and other flags that catch entire classes of bugs at compile time.

rule.desc: Annotate return types on exported functions and public methods.
rule.rationale: Explicit return types prevent accidental API changes, improve documentation, and speed up type checking in large codebases.

rule.desc: Prefer `interface` for object shapes that may be extended; use `type` for unions, intersections, mapped types, and primitives.
rule.rationale: Interfaces support declaration merging and are more performant for object type checking. Types are required for unions and complex type algebra.

rule.desc: Use generics for reusable components that work with multiple types. Constrain type parameters with `extends` when possible.
rule.rationale: Generics provide type safety without sacrificing reusability. Constraints communicate intent and catch misuse.

rule.desc: Use built-in utility types (`Partial`, `Required`, `Pick`, `Omit`, `Record`, `Readonly`) instead of manual type construction.
rule.rationale: Utility types are well-tested, readable, and keep types DRY. They automatically track changes to the source type.

rule.desc: Use literal types and `as const` assertions for values that must be specific constants.
rule.rationale: Literal types and const assertions narrow types at compile time, enabling exhaustive checking and preventing invalid values.

rule.desc: Use discriminated unions with a shared literal property for type-safe variants.
rule.rationale: Discriminated unions enable exhaustive pattern matching, clear control flow, and compiler-enforced completeness.

rule.desc: Use branded types to distinguish semantically different values that share a primitive representation.
rule.rationale: Branded types prevent mixing up IDs, currencies, units, and other domain values that are all strings or numbers at runtime.

rule.desc: Use template literal types for string patterns that follow a known format.
rule.rationale: Template literal types encode string structure in the type system, catching malformed strings at compile time.

rule.desc: Prefer union types or `as const` objects over TypeScript enums. Use enums only when numeric reverse mapping is required.
rule.rationale: Enums generate runtime code, have surprising behaviors (numeric enums are not type-safe, const enums are fragile across module boundaries), and union types are simpler and more predictable.
rule.tool_support: ESLint core no-restricted-syntax rule with TSEnumDeclaration selector can ban enum declarations.
